title: Daily Briefing — Mar 20
headline: Extreme fear, rising volatility, and tech mean reversion—patience wins today.
body: Right, so we've officially entered an extreme fear regime with the Fear & Greed Index at 15.8 and VIX punching up to 25.22, well above its 20-day average. Markets are peeling away from their 20 MAs across the board—SPY ($659.80 vs SMA20 $677.72), QQQ ($593.02 vs SMA20 $604.31), and tech heavies like AAPL, MSFT, META. Volatility is expanding—don’t chase, don’t predict.

Energy (XLE $59.36), Industrials (XLI $164.06), and Real Estate (XLRE $41.92) remain resilient, leading sector rotation with low risk profiles. Tech (XLK $138.43) is improving but still below key MAs, while Financials (XLF $48.99) shows elevated risk but some momentum—watch the mean reversion zone. Defensive sectors (Staples, Healthcare) lag despite rising volatility.

Sentiment is the real contrarian signal here. When Fear & Greed < 25, historical edge is clear: opportunity for patient buyers, but let price confirm. Nearly all tickers and major ETFs are below their 20 MA or approaching, so per rule R006, exit longs or sit on hands—less trades = more wins. If it’s in the press, it’s in the price; ignore consensus panic, track the flows.
callout_body:
SPY: $658.02 (SMA200) support — watch for weekly close
QQQ: $591.74 (SMA200) support
XLE: $59.36 (SMA20 $56.60) — sector rotation leader
XLF: $48.99 (SMA200 $52.58) — financials can't break
NVDA: $178.26 (SMA200) — danger zone if breached
VIX: 25+ — expanding risk regime
Energy names (AR, OIH, SM): All above 20/50 MA — momentum intact
action_items: Reduce equity exposure unless price reclaims daily 20 MA (R006) — stay defensive until confirmed reversal.
Watch XLE and Industrials for sector rotation trades; avoid chasing tech until leadership resumes and MAs are reclaimed.
closing_quote: Extreme fear = opportunity, but patience is king. Follow the flows—let price show you the next trade.
